title: Faction action (P8, Sprint 02)
description: A modder-authored rule for how a player action shifts the player's standing with one or more factions. The engine never names a content id from this contract: action ids live in the mod file (and become a denylist for the architecture guard), the trigger vocabulary is closed, the deltas are numbers. `faction_delta` and `rival_faction_delta` keys are axis names (the canonical three: trust, contribution, notoriety; mods may add axes via `extra`). GAME-DESIGN.md §5.

id.description: The action's unique id. Filename must match (validate_mod_data.py enforces it).

description.description: Human-readable: what the action means in plain language.

trigger.description: The closed trigger vocabulary. The engine dispatches `Reputation.trigger(name, context)` from each hot path; a FactionAction whose trigger matches fires.

applies_to.description: Which faction receives `faction_delta`. 'controlling_faction' (default) is the faction_control of the location where the trigger fired; 'target_faction' is named below; 'all_known' broadcasts.

target_faction_id.description: Required when applies_to is 'target_faction'. Ignored otherwise.

faction_delta.description: Per-axis deltas applied to the affected faction. Keys: trust / contribution / notoriety (canonical); mods may extend with any string. Values clamp to [-100, 100].

rival_faction_id.description: The rival faction. If omitted, the engine picks the faction whose authored `relationships` entry toward `applies_to` is the worst (hostile > tense > neutral > friendly > allied).

rival_faction_delta.description: Per-axis deltas applied to the rival faction. Same shape as faction_delta.

price_modifier.description: Per-axis coefficient in [-0.10, 0.10] applied to the controlling faction's market price (display and charged). Total modifier clamped to [-0.25, 0.25]. positive=price goes UP for friendly standing, DOWN for hostile (e.g. contribution +1 with corp_charter makes corp_charter's goods cheaper).

unlocks.description: Good ids that become buyable when the affected faction's `trust` is >= 25 AND `notoriety` is <= 50. Modders can author new restricted goods this way; the engine only checks the gate.

requires_standing.description: Per-axis minimum (trust) or maximum (notoriety) the player must hold for the action to fire. Omitted axes are unconstrained. Action skipped (with a log line) when the gate fails — it does NOT consume a charge.

extra.description: Free-form escape hatch for mod-specific data. Never read by the engine.
